free_text: Fission fragment angular distributions in
pre-actinide nuclei
Central University of Kerala, Kasaragod
CS-6/1, Golf Green, Kolkata
15UD Pelletron Accelerator.
Two silicon PIPS detectors with active area
100 mm2, thickness 300 um, bias +60 V, were mounted
at laboratory angle 10 degree with respect to beam
direction in horizontal plane and at a distance of
70.0 cm from  the target.
Nine hybrid telescope detectors in two
groups, were mounted on the two movable arms inside the
scattering chamber: six telescopes on the upper arm
covering angle of 60-170 degree and three telescopes
on the lower arm covering angle of 41-70 degree. Each
telescope consisted of an ionization chamber for
measuring energy loss (delta E) of reaction products
in the front followed by a silicon detector to record
the residual enrgy (E) of the same at the back.
No detail information given.
Compiled by Niraj Kr. Rai+BL
